title: The Story That Says Property Is Not for Me
subtitle: A journal prompt set for naming the fears around large-scale wealth creation and finding the counter-evidence already in your life
excerpt: A four-prompt journal set from Day 72 of a 12-week money programme. The prompts move from naming the stories and fears that say property or large-scale wealth is too big, toward finding the specific places in your life where you have already built, attracted, or managed something of lasting value.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 72 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

content.text: A four-prompt journal set from Day 72 of a 12-week money programme. Day 72 theme: trusting the ability to build legacy properties and assets. The prompts move from naming the stories that say property is not for you, toward finding the specific evidence already in your life that proves otherwise. Set a timer. Write without editing.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
When you first imagine owning significant property or building large-scale wealth, what do you feel? Where does that feeling sit?
Write what comes up, whether it's excitement, a quiet no, a blank, or something you can't name yet.
What stories or fears still tell you that property ownership or large-scale wealth creation is too big for you?
Name the story. Where did it come from? Whose voice is it?
Where have you already proven your ability to build, manage, or attract something of lasting value?
Name the thing specifically. Small counts. What you built, what you managed, what you held together.
What experiences have already shown you that you can build or attract something of lasting value?
Write the one example that feels most true. What does it tell you about what is actually possible?
Adapted from the Day 72 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
